description: Stable machine-consumed event types for audit event classification.

codes.summary: Records the opening of a local session and the schema bundle versions used by participating components.
Records a model-gateway outbound request with allowlist and destination attribution.
Records auth-gateway outbound activity with attributable destination and allowlist context.
Records git-gateway remote-mutation outbound activity with attributable destination and allowlist context.
Records external-anchor submission outbound activity with attributable target digest identity, allowlist context, and exact-action bindings.
Records successful artifact ingestion after canonicalization and immutable metadata assignment.
Records deterministic denial of an artifact flow that is not allowed by the signed flow matrix.
Records excerpt promotion request, approval, denial, rate-limit, size-limit, or revocation actions.
Records per-role or per-step artifact quota violations including the rejected operation context.
Records retention, GC, export, and restore actions including reclaimed bytes when applicable.
Records persisted typed policy decision digests with audit binding metadata.
Records local admission of imported historical sealed audit segments without rewriting imported bytes.
Records local admission of restored sealed audit segments from backup/export evidence without rewriting segment bytes.
Records issuance of a secrets access lease with non-secret lease identity and scope context.
Records renewal of a secrets access lease when consumer, scope, and policy context still match the active lease binding.
Records deterministic denial of secrets lease issue, renew, or revoke operations under fail-closed policy and lifecycle checks.
Records expiry of a secrets access lease identity without disclosing secret material.
Records normal release, expiry, or revocation of a secrets access lease without secret values.
Records creation of an isolate session used for TOFU and later attestable signer binding.
Records binding evidence between an isolate session and signer identity for event admissibility checks.
